name: Kahlua Marbled Pumpkin Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine gingersnap and graham cracker crumbs with powdered sugar and butter. Toss to combine. Spread evenly onto bottom of 8-inch springform pan. Bake at 350 degrees F for 5 minutes. Cool.
2. In a mixer bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth. Gradually add granulated sugar and beat until light.
3. Add eggs, one at a time, beating until well combined after each addition.
4. Transfer 1 cup of the mixture to a separate bowl and blend in pumpkin, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, and Kahlua.
5. Pour half of the pumpkin mixture into the prepared crust. Top with half of the cream cheese mixture.
6. Repeat layers using the remaining pumpkin and cream cheese mixtures.
7. Using a table knife, cut through the layers with an upward motion in four to five places to create a marbled effect.
8. Place on a baking sheet and bake at 350 degrees F for 45 minutes.
9. Without opening the oven door, let the cake stand in the turned off oven for 1 hour. Remove from the oven and cool. Then chill. Remove from the pan.
